digest.top_line: NRG/RTOG 0848: adjuvant chemoRT after chemo missed OS overall (HR 0.96, p=0.38); node-negative subgroup gained 5yr OS 48.1% vs 28.6% (hypothesis-generating).
digest.tldr: Upper-GI carried the only signal: NRG/RTOG 0848 adds adjuvant CXRT after gemcitabine-based chemo in resected pancreatic cancer, missing OS overall (HR 0.96) but showing a node-negative subgroup OS gain (interaction p=0.022). Hypothesis-generating on a pre-FOLFIRINOX backbone; adjuvant chemoRT stays off the standard pathway.

sites.intro: Resected pancreatic adenocarcinoma: does consolidative chemoRT after adjuvant chemo earn a role over chemo alone?

studies.name: NRG Oncology/RTOG 0848
studies.tldr: Adjuvant chemoRT did not improve OS overall (HR 0.96, p=0.38); node-negative subgroup gained OS (5yr 48.1% vs 28.6%).
details: 📊 1° EP negative — OS, all pts (N=354): median 2.6 vs 2.3 yr, HR 0.96 (90% CI 0.79-1.18), p=0.38 (1-sided log-rank)
🔍 Phase III RT randomization: post-resection + completed gemcitabine-based adjuvant chemo, then randomized ± fluoropyrimidine-sensitized CXRT
⚠️ Node-negative OS benefit is a subgroup signal (interaction p=0.022); hypothesis-generating, needs confirmation
📊 Node-positive (N1, n=263): no signal — 5yr OS 21.2% vs 20.9%
⚠️ Gemcitabine-based backbone (pre-FOLFIRINOX); generalizability to current mFOLFIRINOX adjuvant standard uncertain
⚠️ RT dose / fractionation / target volume not reported in source text
🔗 Consistent with ESPAC-1's case against adjuvant chemoRT; modern adjuvant SOC is chemo alone (mFOLFIRINOX, PRODIGE-24)

verdict.rationale: Definitive phase III negative on primary OS; reinforces chemo-alone adjuvant standard. Node-negative benefit is a subgroup signal, not enough to change practice.
verdict.audience: Resected pancreatic head adenocarcinoma, post-gemcitabine adjuvant chemo

open_questions: Does node-negative chemoRT benefit hold on a modern FOLFIRINOX adjuvant backbone?
Independent confirmation of OS benefit in node-negative resected pancreatic adenocarcinoma

related_trials.brief_title: Preoperative mFOLFIRINOX (or Gem-Nab-P) +/- Isotoxic High-dose SBRT for Borderline Resectable Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma
related_trials.brief_summary: Surgical resection is the only potentially curative treatment for patients with pancreatic cancer with the aim of curative R0 resection and related improvement of survival. As a standard, surgery is usually followed by adjuvant therapy that improves survival but neoadjuvant therapy (NAT) is a rapidly emerging concept that needs to be explored and validated in terms of therapeutic options in borderline resectable pancreatic tumors. In this setting, preoperative FFX seems to be feasible and can…

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Chemoradiotherapy Versus Chemotherapy for Pancreatic Cancer (ADJUPANC)
related_trials.brief_summary: In this trial, we aim to compare the outcomes of adjuvant chemoradiotherapy with chemotherapy for patients with resected pancreatic cancer who are at high risk of disease progressions.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Aged 18-75 years
* Pathologically confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
* Pathologically confirmed lymph node metastasis, R1 or R2 resection or lymphovascular invasion (one of the risk factors)
* No history of cancer treatment after surgical resection
* No disease…

related_trials.brief_title: Radiotherapy With GX Regimen as Adjuvant Therapy for High-risk Patients Following Pancreatic Cancer Surgery
related_trials.brief_summary: Pancreatic cancer is a highly fatal malignant tumor. Simple surgical operations can no longer meet the treatment needs of pancreatic cancer patients. Postoperative adjuvant chemotherapy has a significant effect, which can effectively prevent or delay tumor recurrence and prolong the overall survival period of pancreatic cancer patients. Based on this, many guidelines both at home and abroad actively recommend that pancreatic cancer patients receive adjuvant chemotherapy after surgery (if there…
